title: oxalis subacaulis
intro: Oxalis subacaulis, also known as the dwarf sorrel or creeping wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalis genus. It is a native plant to the Western Cape Province of South Africa, and its leaves, stems, and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating kidney stones and urinary tract issues. The plant contains oxalic acid, which has been shown to have potential health benefits, including acting as a diuretic, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Oxalis subacaulis is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
